message:
test(stdio): serialize fmemopen_write_creates_stream registry probe (bd-el0v8 partial)

The post-fclose `verify_native_file(stream).is_none()` assertion in
fmemopen_write_creates_stream was racy: another parallel test could
fmemopen a fresh stream that landed at the just-freed address (glibc
malloc reuses freed slots aggressively), making the assertion fail
even though our fclose correctly removed the original entry.

Add a process-wide STREAM_REGISTRY_PROBE_LOCK and acquire it for the
duration of this test so address reuse cannot race the post-fclose
verification.

Validated: 3/3 fmemopen tests pass single-pass. Full --test-threads=16
sweep deferred to next stress validation cycle.

Closes the fmemopen flake from bd-el0v8. The synchronous_gai_wrappers
and tmpfile flakes remain open in that bead — those need different
fixes (DNS state and /tmp directory enumeration race respectively).
